title: Newest Eva Exhibit Currently in Yokohama July 19 to Sept 7
display_title: Newest Eva Exhibit Currently in Yokohama July 19 to Sept 7
wikitext:
[]: Newest Eva Exhibit - Currently in Yokohama July 19 to Sept 7             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Jul 22, 2013 10:49 am http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-2431.html On display will be: 1300 items from NME production 300 of them originals 30 will be from Q There's bound to be a ton of great Qnfo. I wish I could go, but NEET life chose me. My only hope is the fact that the exhibit will travel around Japan until end of 2014. Locations after the exhibit leaves Matsuya Ginza store on August 26th: 2013/10/26-12/27：Fukuoka - Kitakyushu Manga Museum 2014/02/15-04/20：Niigata - Niigata Manga Animation Museum 2014/04/26-05/20：Nagoya - Matsuzakaya Museum 2014/06/25-07/14：Osaka - Hankyu Umeda Gallery 2014/07/19-09/07：Yokohama - Sogo Museum of Art 2014/11/29-2015/01/12：Yamaguchi - Ube Tokiwa Lake Hall Will also feature 1/1 scale ReiQ floating in tang:

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jul 22, 2013 11:50 am Sounds cool! I'm sure Minna No Eva Fan will have a huge report all about it when it happens.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon Jul 22, 2013 1:25 pm The images are all broken for me, but at least I can access the link at the top of the OP. Thoes neweva.blog links have always caused problems for me for some reason....

[]:             Quote Post by Yaywalter » Mon Jul 22, 2013 3:13 pm Sounds awesome, I'll try to make it there if my work schedule allows.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 24, 2013 11:45 am Looks like they'll be a Rei figure from that promotional image available for sale there: http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-2435.html Dunno about the other pilots.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Wed Jul 24, 2013 3:05 pm When will the Gainax/Khara museum open so we can go? ;)

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Aug 06, 2013 2:57 pm Soon to be immersed in Tang I assume https://twitter.com/eva_exhibition/status/364767556214607872/photo/1

[]:             Quote Post by thegoodson » Tue Aug 06, 2013 4:29 pm So what are the chances we get a little morsel of Final news at the opening? Is this pretty much gap-padding or is there a chance?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Aug 06, 2013 5:19 pm Given how the previous exhibition have given nothing at all of future works and only concentrated on what's long been done I wouldn't get my hopes up about any announcements.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Aug 06, 2013 6:37 pm Shooting for a trip to Japan next year in October but who knows where this'll be then.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Aug 12, 2013 8:10 am I'm not sure if I'm interpreting this right but apperently there is a huge book of genga collection being offered. http://twitpic.com/d7m1d4 I very desperately need to get my hands on this. EDIT: This is definitely looking like an actual product being offered

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Aug 12, 2013 10:52 am Maybe it covers what's on display at the exhibit.

[]:             Quote Post by iihieiii » Mon Aug 12, 2013 9:23 pm I was watching this event on the facebook and in the official website along time ago...but since it was in another language i didn't post earlier =P

[]:             Quote Post by CatGirl » Tue Aug 13, 2013 9:09 am I really wish I could go, just to even see the lifesize Rei *___*

[]:             Quote Post by Mbryo » Tue Aug 13, 2013 9:57 am Fuck do I wanna go. I can't..................... Maybe it stays till December next year................but it probably won't.

[]: The Evangelion Exhibition 2013             Quote Post by pen-pen » Thu Aug 15, 2013 7:59 pm The Evangelion Exhibition, to be held from the 7 – 26th August 2013 at the Matsuya Ginza Building ( 8th floor, Event Square ) will feature over 300 pieces of original Genga pieces from the latest Evangelion film, as well as storyboards, layout boards and more. Click link for more info: [url]http://cdn.halcyonrealms.com/animation/the-evangelion-exhibition-2013/[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Black Swordsman » Sat Aug 17, 2013 4:25 pm I might be in Japan next year, wonder if this would still be there...would be cool.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Wed Aug 21, 2013 9:29 am Some news courtesy of Minna no Eva Fan http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-2494.html The attendance has reached 100,000 people in only 15 days of the exhibit being open. As mentioned earlier the exhibit will travel across the country until end of 2014. The next locations for the exhibit: 2013年10月26日-12月27日：福岡 北九州漫画ミュージアム 2014年04月26日-05月20日：名古屋 松坂屋美術館 2014年06月25日-07月14日：大阪 阪急うめだギャラリー 2014年07月19日-09月07日：横浜 そごう美術館 2014年11月29日-2015年01月12日：山口 宇部市ときわ湖水ホール

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Wed Aug 21, 2013 10:18 am I would miss it for the times I am thinking of going. No worldwide tour until 2015 at least.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Aug 22, 2013 5:12 pm Since I wasn't able to google up the info I needed I decided to just try and get the exhibit catalog book myself. Today it has finally arrived into my hands and it is glorious. I can say it is my new favorite thing. The book is sold both at the Exhibit itself (currently at the Matsuya Ginza department store) but is also available from the Matsuya Ginza website: http://www.welci.jp/matsuya/products/detail.php?product_code=62502-EV14 While as far as I could tell they don't ship outside Japan, I have managed to harass a friend in Japan to grab one and send it to me. Anyway, here's what's inside: More than half of the of the book (111 out of 196 pages) is taken up by the Real Scale Gallery where the various materials are beautifully reproduced in their actual size and full color. This section is done of fairly light weight paper with a matte finish which I guess is done to reproduce the full look of the originals but this leads to things being printed only on one side of the page. A few sample photos  SPOILER: Show
